Title: Genomic signatures of vegetable and oilseed allopolyploid Brassica juncea and genetic loci controlling the accumulation of glucosinolates

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulatory genes controlling seed aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation; two major GWAS loci on A02 and A09
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed glucosinolate accumulation; copy loss/CNV at A02 and A09 is associated with low glucosinolate content in oilseed accessions
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glucosinolates (primarily aliphatic glucosinolates, with sinigrin as dominant component)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense compounds; high seed levels are undesirable in oilseed meal due to anti-nutritional, oncogenic and toxicity effects
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: T84-66; AU213; diversity panel of 183 accessions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS using 689,411 filtered gene-space SNPs in 183 accessions; glucosinolate profiling by HPLC; copy number variation analysis from chromosome-scale genomes; ONT long-read mapping validation; prior targeted silencing evidence cited for MYB28 homolog
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS for seed total glucosinolate content identified two major loci on chromosomes A02 and A09 containing MYB28 (HAG1) orthologues. 2. BjuVA02G46870.1 and BjuVA09G07110.1 are major regulators of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis in B. juncea seeds. 3. Accessions with low glucosinolate levels are associated with SNP variation and copy number loss at these MYB28 loci. 4. One copy was lost on both A02 and A09 in the low-glucosinolate oilseed accession AU213, and ONT long reads validated the CNVs. 5. The A09 association is newly reported in B. juncea in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value targets for developing canola-quality B. juncea with low seed glucosinolates; selection or editing of loss-of-function/low-copy MYB28 alleles at both A02 and A09 should reduce seed glucosinolates; useful for marker-assisted selection, genomic selection, and stacking with low erucic acid alleles
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 183 accessions; HPLC metabolite profiling of seed glucosinolates; chromosome-scale genome assemblies and ONT long-read resequencing. BioProject/SRA: PRJNA697823
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes MYB28 control of seed glucosinolate content in B. juncea as novel and not directly inferable from B. napus; A02 peak was unusually broad, consistent with recent introgression history; a 4827 bp deletion covered BjuVA02G46870.1 in AU213
